vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:deft-directive-build
vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for the Lean Angle premium feature (testing phase, IAP deferred)

narratives.Overview: Add a premium Lean Angle feature to MotoSpeedy: a real-time, rear-axial motorcycle indicator that tilts with the bike, a signed live lean angle in degrees from vertical (negative = left, positive = right), and per-session max-left / max-right tracking for track days. The feature is gated behind a lifetime in-app purchase, but during pre-launch testing it is force-unlocked via a single entitlement flag so the full experience is usable without the App Store.
narratives.Problem: Riders — especially track-day riders — want to see and record how far they lean. MotoSpeedy currently only surfaces GPS speed and trip stats. A lean-angle readout plus session max is a differentiated, monetizable feature that uses on-device motion sensors with no additional privacy cost.
narratives.Goals:
1. Show live lean angle derived from the device IMU (CoreMotion gravity vector).
2. Display a logo-style rear-view motorcycle graphic that leans side to side in real time.
3. Show signed degrees from vertical (negative left / positive right).
4. Track and display max-left and max-right lean for the active session.
5. Allow calibration so 'upright' accounts for phone mount angle.
6. Gate the feature behind a lifetime App Store purchase, greyed out until unlocked.
7. During testing, default the feature to unlocked behind a single swappable flag so no App Store setup is required yet.
narratives.NonGoals: In-app purchase wiring with StoreKit 2 and App Store Connect product setup are deferred to a follow-up scope vBRIEF for product launch. This scope ships the full feature behind a testing-unlock flag plus the greyed-out gating UI so the IAP swap is a localized change. Also out of scope: lean-angle history log, export, and Apple Watch.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: MUST compute lean angle from CoreMotion device-motion gravity, with upright = 0 degrees.
FR-2: MUST display a rear-view motorcycle graphic that rotates with the live lean angle, pivoting at the tire contact patch.
FR-3: MUST display the live lean angle as a signed integer in degrees (negative left, positive right).
FR-4: MUST track and display the session max-left and max-right lean angles.
FR-5: MUST only record max lean while moving above a minimum speed (~5 mph) to avoid stationary noise.
FR-6: MUST provide calibration that captures the current orientation as upright (0 degrees) and persists the offset across launches.
FR-7: MUST allow the rider to reset the recorded max lean (also reset alongside Reset Trip semantics).
FR-8: MUST gate the feature behind an entitlement: greyed-out toggle in Settings and hidden ride-screen UI when locked.
FR-9: MUST default to unlocked during the testing phase via a single flag, with no StoreKit dependency.
FR-10: MUST persist the user's enable/disable choice across launches.
narratives.NonFunctionalRequirements:
NFR-1: Lean readings MUST be smoothed (exponential filter) so the indicator is stable under engine vibration.
NFR-2: Lean values MUST be clamped to a physically plausible range (+/-90 degrees) to reject sensor glitches.
NFR-3: Core lean logic MUST be unit-testable without live motion hardware.
NFR-4: The entitlement abstraction MUST make the future StoreKit swap a localized change (single store class).
NFR-5: Motion updates MUST stop when the app is backgrounded to conserve battery.
NFR-6: The feature MUST integrate with the existing Day/Night/Amber theming.
narratives.Architecture:
Mirrors the existing MVVM-ish separation:
- LeanAngleEngine: pure Swift — gravity-vector-to-degrees, calibration offset, exponential smoothing, max-left/right tracking with a moving-speed gate (fully unit-tested).
- LeanAngleService: wraps CMMotionManager device-motion updates (~30 Hz), publishes gravity samples.
- LeanAngleEntitlementStore: ObservableObject gating the feature. testingUnlockOverride = true during pre-launch; purchase()/restorePurchases() are StoreKit-ready stubs.
- LeanAngleViewModel: bridges service + engine, pulls current speed from RideViewModel for the moving-speed gate, persists calibration to AppSettings.
- LeanAngleView: rear-view motorcycle SwiftUI Shape that rotates with live lean, signed degrees readout, max L/R stat tiles, Calibrate + Reset Max controls; theme-aware.
- AppSettings: persists leanAngleEnabled and leanCalibrationOffset.
- AppModel: owns the entitlement store + view model, starts/stops motion on scene phase and settings changes, wires the speed provider.
- RideView: shows the gated lean section (unlocked AND enabled); SettingsView: greyed-out Lean Angle toggle.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
Phase 1 — Core (done)
- LeanAngleEngine + LeanAngleState + unit tests.
- LeanAngleService (CoreMotion).
- LeanAngleEntitlementStore (testing-unlocked, IAP-ready stubs).
- LeanAngleViewModel bridging service/engine/speed.
- AppSettings persistence (enabled + calibration offset).
- LeanAngleView + RideView/SettingsView integration with greyed-out gating.

Phase 2 — Launch (deferred, separate scope)
- StoreKit 2 non-consumable purchase + restore + entitlement persistence.
- App Store Connect product, privacy answers, screenshots, review notes.
- Flip testingUnlockOverride to false.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit tests for LeanAngleEngine: sign convention (left negative / right positive), gravity-to-degrees, exponential smoothing toward target, convergence over many samples, max-left/right tracking, moving-speed gate, +/-90 clamping, calibration offset subtraction, reset-max preserving calibration. Manual device testing: phone flat (0 degrees), tilt left/right, calibrate while mounted, reset max, ride at speed for realistic max capture, and verify motion stops in background. Entitlement: verify greyed-out gating when locked by flipping the testing flag, and full availability when unlocked.
narratives.Deployment: Ships in the standard Xcode build → TestFlight → App Store pipeline. No backend. CoreMotion needs no Info.plist usage string. Lifetime IAP product and StoreKit wiring land in the deferred launch scope.
narratives.Risk: Lean accuracy depends on a stable phone mount and correct calibration. Sensor noise/vibration is mitigated by smoothing and clamping. The deferred IAP means the feature is free during testing; the testing-unlock flag must be flipped before launch to avoid giving the premium feature away.
narratives.OpenQuestions:
1. Final IAP price tier (typical one-time unlock $2.99–$9.99).
2. Whether to offer a free trial vs paid-only at launch.
3. Custom rear-view motorcycle asset vs the current SwiftUI vector shape.
4. Whether lean should freeze or hide below the moving-speed threshold (currently shown live, max not recorded).

items.title: LeanAngleEngine + state + unit tests (calibration, smoothing, clamping, max L/R)
